seo.title: LlamaIndex Guardrails for Recruitment/HR
seo.description: Compliance guide for LlamaIndex agents in recruitment/hr. Meet EEOC guidelines, NYC Local Law 144 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Recruitment/HR teams using LlamaIndex for resume screening and candidate matching face heightened compliance risks due to strict anti-discrimination laws like EEOC guidelines and the EU AI Act. Retrieval failures or hallucinations can lead to biased recommendations, discriminatory hiring decisions, and regulatory violations. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify LlamaIndex outputs against compliance standards, ensuring fair and auditable AI-driven hiring processes.

complianceRequirements.description: Prohibits AI tools from screening candidates based on protected characteristics like race, gender, age, or disability
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Verifies query responses exclude references to protected attributes and flags biased language in resume rankings

complianceRequirements.description: Requires bias audits for automated employment decision tools and candidate notification of AI usage
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Automates annual compliance checks on LlamaIndex retrievals and generates audit trails for notifications

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates risk management, transparency, and human oversight for hiring AI systems
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Monitors index freshness and retrieval relevance to prevent high-risk failures in candidate evaluation

complianceRequirements.description: Requires reasonable accommodations and prohibits discrimination based on disability or other protected classes
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Detects hallucinated disqualifications and ensures chunked resume context preserves accommodation requests

industryRisks.name: Discriminatory Candidate Rejection
industryRisks.description: Irrelevant retrieval pulls biased historical data, rejecting qualified diverse candidates
industryRisks.example: LlamaIndex retrieves outdated 'cultural fit' docs favoring specific demographics

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated Disqualification
industryRisks.description: Synthesizer invents reasons for rejection beyond resume content
industryRisks.example: AI claims 'leadership gap' for mid-career return-to-work mothers

industryRisks.name: Outdated Job Requirement Responses
industryRisks.description: Stale index serves old qualification criteria, rejecting valid candidates
industryRisks.example: Pre-2024 index rejects remote workers despite new hybrid policy

industryRisks.name: Context Loss from Chunking
industryRisks.description: Critical accommodation requests split across chunks get ignored
industryRisks.example: Disability disclosure in resume footer missed by retrieval

implementation.title: Wrap Query Engine with Vex Verification
implementation.description: Protects candidate screening by verifying retrieval relevance and bias-free responses
implementation.code:
from llama_index.core import VectorStoreIndex, SimpleDirectoryReader
from vex_sdk import verify

# Load resumes into LlamaIndex
documents = SimpleDirectoryReader('resumes/').load_data()
index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(documents)
query_engine = index.as_query_engine()

# Wrap with Vex for HR compliance
def safe_candidate_screening(query):
    response = query_engine.query(query)
    verified = verify(
        response.response,
        rules={
            'no_protected_attributes': 'response must not reference age, gender, race, or disability',
            'relevant_to_query': 'retrieved context directly answers hiring criteria',
            'no_hallucinations': 'all claims supported by source documents'
        }
    )
    return verified

result = safe_candidate_screening('Find software engineers with 3+ years Python experience')

implementation.title: Index Freshness & Audit Monitoring
implementation.description: Prevents stale data violations with automated compliance checks
implementation.code:
from llama_index.core import StorageContext, load_index_from_storage
from vex_sdk import verify
import datetime

def audit_index_compliance(index_path: str):
    # Load existing index
    storage_context = StorageContext.from_defaults(persist_dir=index_path)
    index = load_index_from_storage(storage_context)
    
    # Check freshness (must be updated within 30 days for audits)
    last_updated = datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp(index.storage_context.persist_dir.stat().st_mtime)
    if (datetime.datetime.now() - last_updated).days > 30:
        raise ValueError('Index stale - violates annual audit requirements')
    
    # Vex compliance scan
    test_query = 'Rank candidates for Data Scientist role'
    response = index.as_query_engine().query(test_query)
    
    audit_result = verify(
        response.response,
        rules={
            'bias_audit_passed': 'no discriminatory patterns detected',
            'eu_ai_act_compliant': 'transparent reasoning with source citations',
            'complete_context': 'no chunk boundary losses affecting decisions'
        },
        metadata={'audit_date': datetime.datetime.now().isoformat()}
    )
    
    return audit_result

# Run monthly compliance audit
audit_index_compliance('./hr_resume_index')

caseStudyScenario.heading: LlamaIndex in Recruitment/HR: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
TechCo deployed LlamaIndex to screen 10,000 engineering resumes monthly, indexing candidate documents with VectorStoreIndex for rapid qualification matching. The system ranked candidates by retrieving relevant experience chunks and synthesizing fit scores. However, common LlamaIndex failure modes emerged: irrelevant historical hiring data contaminated retrievals with 'cultural fit' biases, the synthesizer hallucinated leadership gaps for diverse candidates, chunk boundaries split critical accommodation disclosures, and the index grew stale after policy changes allowing remote work.

During EEOC audit preparation, TechCo discovered the AI systematically downranked mid-career women returning from maternity leave and older candidates, citing 'recent experience gaps' not present in resumes. NYC Local Law 144 compliance was impossible without auditable outputs, risking $1,500 fines per violation across thousands of screenings.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Integrating Vex guardrails around query_engine.query() transformed compliance. Vex blocked 87% of biased responses by verifying against EEOC rules and flagging hallucinated disqualifications. Index freshness checks enforced weekly updates, while chunk verification preserved accommodation context. The system now auto-generates NYC Law 144 notices and audit trails, passing external bias audits with 98% compliance. TechCo avoided lawsuits, improved diverse hire rates by 23%, and maintained EEOC compliance across 50,000 screenings.
